commit.message:
Add authenticated RSVP read/upsert endpoints for seeded events (#44)

This PR implements Phase 4 RSVP API support by enabling authenticated
users to read and upsert their RSVP state on existing seeded events. It
adds the missing social RSVP surface area while preserving current event
discovery behavior.

- **API surface (authenticated social endpoints)**
- Added `GET /api/v1/social/rsvps/{eventId}` to fetch the caller’s RSVP
for a specific event.
- Added `PUT /api/v1/social/rsvps/{eventId}` to create-or-update the
caller’s RSVP (`yes|no|maybe`).

- **RSVP domain implementation**
- Introduced `Rsvp` Eloquent model (UUID primary key, `responded_at`
cast).
- Added request validation via `UpsertRsvpRequest` (`response` enum
validation).
- Added `RsvpResource` for stable JSON shape (`userId`, `eventId`,
`response`, `respondedAt`).

- **Controller behavior**
- Added `RsvpController` with event existence checks and user-scoped
lookup/upsert logic.
  - Upsert now returns status by outcome:
    - `201` when RSVP is created
    - `200` when RSVP is updated
  - Returns `404` for missing event / missing RSVP on read.

- **Routing and contract docs**
- Wired RSVP endpoints into existing `auth:sanctum` social route group.
- Added `Social` OpenAPI tag and endpoint annotations for RSVP
read/upsert, including distinct `200`/`201` responses.

- **Feature coverage**
  - Added focused feature tests for:
    - auth required on RSVP read/write
    - create then update semantics on upsert
    - user-scoped RSVP retrieval
    - invalid response validation

```php
Route::middleware('auth:sanctum')->prefix('social')->group(function () {
    Route::get('/rsvps/{eventId}', [RsvpController::class, 'show']);
    Route::put('/rsvps/{eventId}', [RsvpController::class, 'upsert']);
});
```

---------

docs: add `instructions/oversized` to `aipm.toml` JSON schema (#454)

* docs: add instructions/oversized to aipm.toml JSON schema

The instructions/oversized rule was added to the lint system with
configurable options (lines, characters, resolve-imports) but was
never registered in the JSON Schema for aipm.toml. This caused editors
and schema validators to reject valid configurations such as:

  [workspace.lints."instructions/oversized"]
  lines = 200
  characters = 20000
  resolve-imports = true

Add a dedicated patternProperties entry for instructions/oversized
with full documentation for all supported properties:
- level    — severity override (optional)
- lines    — maximum line count (default: 100)
- characters — maximum character count (default: 15000)
- resolve-imports — follow @import chains (default: false)
- ignore   — per-rule path exclusions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* Update schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json

feat(kyverno): add 5 core DevSecOps ClusterPolicies

- disallow-privileged-containers (Enforce) - blocks privileged: true
- disallow-privilege-escalation (Enforce) - requires allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
- require-resource-limits (Audit) - CPU/memory limits and requests required
- disallow-latest-image-tag (Enforce) - blocks :latest and untagged images
- require-non-root-user (Audit) - containers must not run as root

Critical security policies use Enforce; behavioural policies use Audit
to allow assessment before hard enforcement. System namespaces (kube-system,
crossplane-system, argocd, kyverno, monitoring) are excluded.

Policies applied via kubectl_manifest after helm_release.kyverno in platform.tf.

commit.message:
fix: resolve stylix HM double-import build error

- Fix attribute name: homeModules.default → homeManagerModules.default
  nixpalette-hyprland only exposes homeManagerModules, not homeModules
- Set stylix.homeManagerIntegration.autoImport = false
  Stylix's NixOS module auto-injects homeModules.stylix into
  home-manager.sharedModules by default. Our explicit sharedModules
  addition (nixpalette-hyprland → nixpalette → stylix HM module) would
  load the same stylix lambda a second time, causing the read-only
  stylix.base16 option to be set twice.
  Disabling autoImport ensures the stylix HM module is loaded exactly
  once, via nixpalette-hyprland.homeManagerModules.default.
  nixpalette's hm.nix independently sets all required stylix options
  from the resolved theme, so followSystem is not needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(skills): refactor task-implementation-flow to pure orchestrator pattern

Rewrite the task-implementation-flow skill across all AI tool directories
(.claude, .cursor, .codex) to adopt a pure orchestrator model where the
main invoker never executes actions directly — it only spawns dedicated
subagents per phase and aggregates their results.

Phase changes:
- Discovery: spawns Discovery Orchestrator → 3 parallel sub-subagents
  (Jira fetch + In Progress transition, codebase analysis, requirements)
- Planning: spawns a Planning Agent that creates the execution plan and
  git worktree
- Development: unchanged in model, explicit parallel-batch subagents
- Validation: spawns 6 parallel Validation Agents (lint, type-check,
  unit tests, integration tests, build, acceptance criteria)
- Delivery: spawns a Delivery Agent (commit → push → PR → worktree
  remove → Jira Code Review transition)

Files updated:
- SKILL.md: fully rewritten with orchestrator architecture, subagent
  prompts, and agent call examples for all 5 phases
- README.md: rewritten with orchestrator model and subagent table
- QUICKSTART.md: updated phase table and progress tracking for
  subagent-based flow
- EXAMPLES.md: all examples rewritten to show spawning language and
  parallel agent outputs

fix: disable prometheusOperator TLS to prevent missing admission secret

When admissionWebhooks.patch.enabled=false the certgen job never runs
and the kube-prometheus-stack-admission TLS secret is never created.
The operator pod was still configured with --web.enable-tls=true and
tried to mount that secret, getting stuck in ContainerCreating — which
in turn meant no Prometheus StatefulSet was created and Grafana had no
datasource to connect to.

Fix: add prometheusOperator.tls.enabled=false to the helm_release values
so the operator starts without requiring the TLS secret.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: mount /api/remotes before /api catch-all for token-auth preview

The preview endpoint uses ?tok= query param auth for <video>/<audio> src
elements that can't send Authorization headers. Since /api catch-all
applies authMw to all routes, /api/remotes must be mounted first so the
pre-authMw preview handler can process token-authenticated requests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

int4 quant, optimizations, 10k context window (#2)

* iOS: fix per-token performance overhead in decode loop

* fix: revert pre-allocated MLMultiArray scalars (CoreML caches by identity)

CoreML caches MLMultiArray input data by object identity. Reusing
pre-allocated scalar arrays caused all prefill chunks to run at
position 0, garbling the KV cache and producing empty responses.

Also removes diagnostic logging from InferenceEngine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* iOS: add [Perf] timing instrumentation to inference engine

Per-chunk prefill timing, per-step decode timing (sampling vs model),
and end-of-generation summary with tokens/sec.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* iOS: add [Perf] logging to eager prefill

Logs per-chunk timing in EagerPrefillManager.prefillNewChunks() and
cache hit/miss in finishPrefill(), filling the gap between generation
[Perf] logs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add MIL pass to fuse decomposed attention into scaled_dot_product_attention

Replace the matmul→select→softmax→matmul pattern (35 instances per
function) with CoreML's native iOS18 scaled_dot_product_attention op.

The SDPA op always applies 1/sqrt(E) scaling, but Gemma4 uses scale=1.0
(QK-norm absorbs scaling). The pass pre-multiplies Q by sqrt(E) to
cancel the built-in divisor: (Q·√E) @ K^T / √E = Q @ K^T.

Pattern matching handles:
- Explicit transpose(K) or matmul transpose_y=True
- Optional cast(fp32) between mask and softmax
- Optional select(bool_mask, scores, -10000) masking
- Backward DCE of dead intermediate ops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix eager prefill thrashing with KV checkpoints

Save KV state at each chunk boundary during eager prefill. When
the user types and only the last chunks change, restore from the
nearest valid checkpoint instead of re-prefilling all chunks from
scratch. Previously, every keystroke caused a full re-prefill
(~8-13s on CPU-only), wasting 100+ seconds per turn.

Also cancel the prefill debounce task when Send is tapped to prevent
a stale textChanged from running concurrently with generation,
which caused CPU contention and slowed decode steps from 0.28s to 2-3s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite SDPA fusion pass to match real StableHLO→MIL graphs

The previous pass matched synthetic MIL patterns but failed on real
graphs produced by stablehlo-coreml. The converter wraps matmuls in
reshape/transpose chains for batch-dim folding.

Key changes:

- Anchor on softmax ops (not matmul) and trace backward/forward through
  reshape/transpose to find the QK and WV matmuls
- Work at rank 4: emit SDPA(Q_4d, K_4d, V_4d, mask) directly,
  eliminating all reshape/transpose wrapping ops
- Pre-scale Q by sqrt(E) to cancel SDPA's built-in 1/sqrt(E)

Pipeline ordering fix (ct_convert_pipeline.py):
- Move cleanup passes (collapse_reshape_chains, remove_redundant_maximum,
  remove_broadcast_tiles) BEFORE fusion passes (softmax, SDPA)

Tests (tests/test_sdpa_fusion.py):
- Trace real JAX attention through StableHLO→MIL pipeline
- Verify SDPA fusion for: masked, unmasked, head_dim=512, GQA
- Verify standalone softmax is NOT fused to SDPA

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KV checkpoint corruption: deep-copy MLMultiArrays

CoreML reuses output MLMultiArray buffers across predictions, so
saving raw references in checkpoints meant subsequent prefill calls
silently overwrote checkpoint data. Deep-copy each KV state when
saving to ensure checkpoints are independent snapshots.

Cost: ~3ms memcpy per checkpoint vs ~1.3s per re-prefill.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SDPA fusion for GQA 8:1 layout; add real-pattern GQA tests

- Peel K/V through reshape only (not transpose) to handle
  GQA batch-fold transposes correctly
- Insert explicit layout transposes: K [H,B,S,E]→[B,H,S,E],
  V [H,B,EV,S]→[B,H,S,EV]
- Add GQA sliding (head_dim=256) and global (head_dim=512) tests
  using real Gemma4 pattern (repeat on axis=2 + transpose)
- All 6 tests pass including both GQA configs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace KV cache jnp.where scatter with dynamic_update_slice in decode

Sliding-window decode was using arange+eq+broadcast+where to write a
single KV slot, copying the entire 512-entry cache. Switch to
dynamic_update_slice (already used for global layers and prefill).
Eliminates ~288 ops per decode step across 12 sliding layers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* int4 group quantization: halve model size (5.05 GB → 2.87 GB)

Replace int8 per-channel quantization with int4 group quantization
(GROUP_SIZE=32) using symmetric blockwise scheme.

Key changes:
- quantize_const_weights.py: rewrite from int8 per-channel to int4 group
  quantization with proper numpy dtype metadata (SUB_BYTE_DTYPE_METADATA_KEY)
  so coremltools serializes as packed 4-bit storage
- export.py: update streaming quantizer to use int4 blockwise function

Parity vs JAX reference (single decode step):
  int8: cosine_sim=0.999725, mean_abs_diff=0.474
  int4: cosine_sim=0.992064, mean_abs_diff=1.820

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mixed-precision quantization, softcap fusion, SDPA scale detection

Quantization:
- Mixed precision: int8 for embedding tables (vocab_size dim), int4 for rest
- Detailed logging: per-tensor precision choice, warning on constexpr re-quant
- Summary: N int4 + M int8 + K skipped

New pass: fuse_logit_softcap
- Fuses tanh(x/cap)*cap → scaled_tanh(x, alpha=cap, beta=1/cap)
- Handles both real_div and mul input forms
- 6 tests in tests/test_softcap_fusion.py

SDPA upstream fix:
- Detect explicit real_div/mul by sqrt(E) between matmul and softmax
- Standard attention (Llama/Mistral): absorb scaling, no Q pre-scaling
- Gemma-style (scale=1.0): keep Q pre-scaling by sqrt(E)
- New test: test_standard_scaled_attention

Also: remove_broadcast_tiles now handles multi-dim tiles

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add MIL optimization passes: GELU fusion, transpose collapse, reduce_mean

Three new passes + one fix, collectively eliminating ~50% of non-const ops:

- replace_erf_gelu: Rewrite pattern matcher for current JAX erf lowering.
  Match abs → mul(sub(0,gate), 1/√2) → Gaussian core signature, find
  0.5*gate half-multiply, replace with native gelu op. 70 fusions per
  function, ~4,550 polynomial ops eliminated each.

- collapse_transpose_chains: Compose consecutive transpose ops.
  Identity compositions (perm∘perm = id) eliminated entirely; others
  collapsed to single transpose. Handles multi-consumer intermediates.
  65 ops eliminated per function.

- fuse_reduce_sum_to_mean: Replace reduce_sum → reshape → mul(1/N) with
  reduce_mean → reshape. Detects uniform-valued tensor constants (not
  just scalars). 242 fusions (prefill) / 207 (decode).

- collapse_reshape_chains: Handle multi-consumer reshape→reshape chains.
  Keep parent (has other users), replace child with direct reshape.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SDPA fusion output layout for GQA prefill (L>1)

The SDPA op always outputs [B,H,L,EV], but GQA attention in Gemma4
produces [B,L,H,EV] after the output reshape-transpose chain. For
decode (L=1) this was a silent no-op; for prefill (L=8, H=8) the
data ordering was wrong, producing garbage logits and empty responses.

Fix: compute a compensating output permutation from the original
reshape-transpose chain and apply it after the SDPA op when it
differs from identity.

Also includes:
- Numerical correctness tests for simple, GQA L=1, and GQA L=8
- _get_scalar() fix in softcap fusion for uniform-valued tensors
- int32 matmul cast fix in decode_coreml.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix streaming quantizer dispatch: patch registry not just class method

The @register_stablehlo_op decorator caches a direct function reference
in _stablehlo_ops_registry at class-definition time. Our monkey-patch
replaced StableHloConverter.op_constant but the dispatch table still
pointed to the original, so the streaming quantizer never fired.

The quantize_const_weights MIL pass was the actual fallback producing
correct int4 weights, so the model was fine — but fixing this allows
streaming quantization to work as intended (lower peak memory).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use int4 for all weights including embeddings

Drop the embedding→int8 special case. All 279 weight tensors now use
int4 group quantization (group_size=32). Model size drops from 4.1 GB
to 2.6 GB. Chat output verified: 'Hi! How can I help you today? 😊'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix BearSSL linking in freestanding mode

Two issues when linking libbearssl.a with -nostdlib:

1. Ubuntu's gcc auto-enables _FORTIFY_SOURCE, inserting calls to
   __memcpy_chk, __memset_chk etc. that don't exist without libc.
   Fix: add -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE to the BearSSL build workflow.

2. BearSSL calls standard libc functions (open, close, read, time,
   getentropy, __errno_location) which don't exist in freestanding
   mode. Fix: add thin syscall-based stubs in osc_runtime.c, guarded
   by OSC_FREESTANDING && __x86_64__ && __linux__.

After this change, re-run the 'Build BearSSL' workflow to rebuild
libbearssl.a without fortified symbols.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: clamp progress to duration and improve queue advance resilience

- Clamp progress_ms <= duration_ms in _send_progress_to_ynison to prevent
  Ynison error 400030001 (progress > duration) which disconnects WebSocket
  and causes queue advance to fail silently
- Wait up to 10s for reconnection in _advance_queue_index before sending
  critical state updates (expanded queue, index advance)
- Trigger prefetch at index >= len-2 (not just == len-2) so the last track
  also triggers prefetch if it wasn't done on the second-to-last

Root cause: PCM byte counter can slightly overshoot track duration at
end-of-stream. Ynison rejects progress=252067 > duration=252000, drops the
WebSocket, and the subsequent queue advance + expanded playlist are lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Support non-homogeneous squad archetypes

Federation squads are no longer limited to scatter-gather deliverables.
Four archetypes supported:

- deliverable: produce file artifacts, meta-squad aggregates (existing)

- research: produce design docs/PRDs, completion = doc approved
- task: execute work items, completion = status update

Meta-squad can manage mixed archetypes simultaneously.

(archetype table + per-type workflows), federation-orchestration skill
